merger_description:
Premier Fresh Australia Pty Ltd (**Premier Fresh**) proposes to purchase 100% of the shares in the following entities that are owned by Carmelo De Domenico and C.A. De Domenico Pty Ltd (the **Vendor**):

(a)        North Queensland IQF Pty Ltd, and

(b)        Double D Produce Pty Ltd

(collectively, the **Domenico Group**),

as well as the business, land and assets owned and operated by the Domenico Group.

The Domenico Group and the Vendor are involved in the growing, packing and value add processing for capsicums located in Ayr, Queensland.

Premier Fresh, through its related entities, has had a long-standing commercial relationship with the Domenico Group, with Premier Fresh historically purchasing and marketing a majority of the Domenico Group’s seasonal capsicum production under a marketing agreement.

Premier Fresh, through its related entities known as the Premier Fresh Group, is a horticulture marketer and supply chain operator that operates across a number of fruit and vegetable supply lines.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Premier Fresh Australia Pty Ltd (Premier Fresh) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
the shares in North Queensland IQF Pty Ltd and Double D Produce
Pty Ltd (collectively, the Domenico Group), as well as the business,
land and assets owned and operated by the Domenico Group, as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.

The acquirer, Premier Fresh, is a horticulture marketer and supply
chain operator that operates across a number of fruit and vegetable
supply lines, including capsicums. Premier Fresh, through its related
entities, has had a long-standing commercial relationship with the
Domenico Group, with Premier Fresh historically purchasing and
marketing a majority of the Domenico Group’s seasonal capsicum
production under a marketing agreement.
The target, the Domenico Group, and the vendor, Carmelo De
Domenico and C.A. De Domenico Pty Ltd, are involved in the growing,
packing and value add processing for capsicums located in
Queensland.
Premier Fresh and the Domenico Group overlap in the supply of
capsicums in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification
thresholds would be met.
The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be
likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the
information provided in the application. It considers that the
Acquisition warrants consideration through the notification process,
including further information gathering and consultation with third
parties. This may include a focus on:
a. the extent of horizontal competitive overlap and the nature
of the vertical relationship between Premier Fresh and the
Domenico Group in the production and supply of capsicums
in Australia is unclear based on the application
b. the potential for foreclosure, noting that Premier Fresh is one
of the largest horticultural wholesalers.
c. the geographic dimensions of the markets
d. the extent to which the parties are likely to be constrained by
alternative producers and suppliers of capsicums in
Australia.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
